back: A blueprint for creating objects. It defines methods and attributes that objects will have.
A reference to the instance of the class. Used inside methods to access instance variables.
The constructor method that initializes an object when it's created.
A way for a class to inherit attributes and methods from another class.
The ability of objects to take multiple forms or the ability to use the same interface for different data types.
Bundling data and methods together and hiding internal details. Use _ or __ prefix for private attributes.

front: What is an instance variable vs class variable?
back: Instance variables belong to individual objects (defined in __init__). Class variables are shared by all instances.
A method that doesn't use self or cls. Defined with @staticmethod. Can be called on class without creating instance.
A method that uses cls instead of self. Defined with @classmethod. Works with the class itself, not the instance.

front: What is a property in Python?
back: Using @property decorator to make methods accessible like attributes. Enables getters/setters: obj.value instead of obj.get_value()
Calls parent class methods from a child class. Used in inheritance to access parent functionality.
Creating a method in a child class with the same name as the parent class. The child version replaces the parent version.
A class from abc module that cannot be instantiated and defines methods subclasses must implement.
Double underscore methods like __str__, __repr__, __len__, __getitem__. Control object behavior in special contexts.

front: What is the difference between is and == for objects?
back: == compares value, is compares identity (same object in memory). For instances, == uses __eq__ method.
